inline_comments.message:
there is another logging class/jar in Policy project, 
        <groupId>org.onap.policy.common</groupId>
        <artifactId>ONAP-Logging</artifactId>
and import it by 
import org.onap.policy.common.logging.flexlogger.FlexLogger;
import org.onap.policy.common.logging.flexlogger.Logger;

which one should we use? we'd better keep it alignment.
is there any dependency between params and params.getPluginHandlerParameters()? like the sequence to register/unregister which one first.

one more thing, from the public static void register(final ParameterGroup parameterGroup) of ParameterService, it will use the parameterGroup.getName() as a key, throw an exception once the key has already existed for register and similar logic for deregister. 
so this function will throw exception since the Name is same 
distributionParameterGroup.getName(). 

and do you think the name "unregister" is better than "deregister"? :)

I followed the same which was being used in the distribution component. And I think we should continue to use slf4j logging throughout the component. 
@Pam/Liam - Is that ok?

No, there is no dependency. Parameter Service takes a group and registers it in a map using its name as key & the group object as value. It has no logic to know about nested objects/maps.
And whenever you provide it a name of the group it will use it as a key to find the relevant object from map and return the same.
So basically, "params" will register one group in Parameter Service and "params.getPluginHandlerParameters()" will register another group.

The getName() method is overridden in ReceptionHandlerParameters & PluginHandlerParameters to create a unique name using the main distribution group name and return the same. Hence, the Parameter Service will never complain about duplicates.

Regarding method name, again I followed the same as in the Parameter Service.:)

Good question!

I have used XLogger because I like its entry/exit methods. The ONAP guidelines are OK with slf4j:
https://wiki.onap.org/display/DW/ONAP+Application+Logging+Guidelines+v1.1#ONAPApplicationLoggingGuidelinesv1.1-SLF4J

However, we should  be guided by @Pam here. It is a small job to change to a standard logging approach now, much more difficult later on.
I guess later we may have to put in some hooks in this method to take down the subscriptions to policy sources such as SDC.

The parameter service is a flat map. Therefore, if you want to register a sub-group of parameters in the parameter service it's name MUST be unique.

This is for convenience. Take a hierarchy where G0->G1.0->G2.0
  ->G1.1->G2.1
        ->G2.2

You can always get to G2.0, G2.3 etc by reading G0 from the parameter service and navigating down the hierarchy to the group you want.


However, it is much more convenient to just go and get G2.2 directly in your class, if you already know the name of the parameter group you need you can get it directly from the parameter service.
Nice! Its beginning to come together!

Is it possible to initialize extra policy decoders or policy forwarders, say for example we implement a REST or CLI interface in the future to allow us to add, remove, or amend the properties of a decoder or forwarder on the fly without taking down and bringing back up the JVM?

You may need a map rather than a collection to hold the forwarders and decoders in order to do the CRUD in a nice way and refer to them by name?

There's a common interface for starting and stopping things in common capabilities called Startable, could we use that instead of this interface even if there are some methods that might not be needed here?

We should use the ONAP-Logging that is in policy/common. Then we are consistent around the repo's.

We need to conform to Logging 1.2 specification - although I don't think we have resources to do so for all the repos. I think Amdocs will help us, but not sure and its getting late in the release process.
got it.
got it. after check the details implementation, yes, there is no duplicate.
I just wanted Ram's opinion on this.
And also on this.
I think that may be done by the respective ReceptionHandler instances using the method "void destroy()" in the ReceptionHandler interface.

I think it will be possible to add/modify/remove the decoder/forwarder at run time using REST or CLI.
For example - one approach will be to build a new collection/map of decoders/forwarders along side and then swap the old one to new one in PluginHandler.

Regarding using map, I feel yes it will better to have map with key as name. But the current interfaces are designed to go with collection.
Also, in case of collection there is only instance of decoder/forwarder but in case of map we can keep the class itself and instantiate it before use every time. I will discuss more with you about it and then we can decide.

I think we can use the Startable interface. 
May be just the start(), shutdown() methods for now. And the stop() method in future when we plan to change the collection/map of decoders/forwarders on fly in future.
I am working on it. Will submit the review soon.
Done. Changed the logging to use ONAP-Logging from policy/common.

I am taking my words back, we cannot use Startable here. 
Reason - The initialize() method needs the parameter group name to  initialize the handlers, decoders & forwarders. But the start() methods in Startable has no parameters. Also, we intend to throw custom exceptions back to the activator which cannot be done using Startable.
